title: 1985 Denver Mini Classic, Boys 10 - 11, Stage 3 Circuit Race

enrichment.summary: A race announcer narrates a 1985 youth cycling circuit race in Denver, Colorado, describing the action as 10-11-year-old boys compete in the final stage of the Mile High Mini Classic. Kyle Pounds finishes fourth in the race behind riders referred to as 'my constant cheetah' (first), Mike Tue Lee (second), and Eric Owen (third).
enrichment.speaker: other

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle Pounds is in fifth place overall at the start of the race.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: The race is the final event of the Mile High Mini Classic weekend.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: The temperature at the start of the race is about 80 degrees.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: The lead car starts the race.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: There are at least four riders in the lead group during the first lap.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle Pounds is in the top four during the race.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: The bell lap indicates one lap remaining for the riders.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: Brittany is leading, followed by a constant cheetah.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: A sprint finish is not expected from the leading riders.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: Michael Hegerty finished second in a previous time trial.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: Eric Owen takes third place in the race.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle Pounds finishes in fourth place.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: Tim Halgen finishes in fifth place.

claims.subject: race
claims.claim: The constant cheetah rider has won two races over the past two days.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle Pounds finished fourth in the original time trial.

transcript.text: Sunday, June 9th, International Meridian Business Park, Denver, Colorado, 1985.
Second day of the first annual Mile High Mini Classic, 10, 11-year-old boys road race at 11 o'clock in the morning.
Kyle Pounds now placed number five overall.
On the left of the screen, the black helmet with the white stripes in the front row in number fifth position.
The boys are lined up getting their instructions for the race this morning.
Temperature about 80 degrees.
This is the final race of this weekend.
Here we go. Lead car starts up.
And they're off on the first lap.
Lyndon Pounds out watching for his brother Kyle to come in on the first lap.
At least four in the lead pack right now.
And that would include my guests, Mike Churley, Mike Constantino, Michael Hegarty, and probably Tim Haugen.
We'll be checking to see if that's right.
And this will be the bell lap for these riders.
They'll have one lap to go when they come around next.
Hey, it's Kyle Pounds in the top four.
He's hanging in there in the top four.
The top four, he's doing it.
Great, Kyle. Great, Kyle.
Hang in there, babe. Hang in there.
One lap to go for the Meridian Park sugar race.
Reading is Mike Churley, followed by Mike Constantino.
Led by our lead motorcycle.
And it looks like we have the same group that we had the previous lap.
The power's in the top four.
It looks like someone has dropped off the end.
It looks like we have three riders right now.
And we'll be looking for a sprint from those gentlemen.
Once again, I've been looking for Mike Churley to finish really well today.
He's also Michael Hegarty, which finished second in the time trial yesterday.
And here they come.
Looks like Mike Constantino is going to pick up another win today, followed by Mike Churley.
So Mike Constantino picks up his second win in the Mini Classic Series today.
And that was Eric Owen taking third place.
Shit. Kyle Pounds is going to come in fourth.
Here comes Kyle Pounds, and he'll take up fourth place from the original group of four
That formed in the beginning of this race.
So once again, we have Mike Constantino taking first place.
Mike Churley in second place.
Eric Owen taking third.
And Kyle Pounds in fourth so far.
Right now, this is Tim Haugen finishing.
And he'll take fifth place.
So Tim, after difficult ride, takes fifth place.
Tim Haugen.
So Mike Constantino is definitely someone to look out for.
Fourth in a really rough race.
Fantastic.
Fantastic.
For the individual time trial, he only finished fourth place.
He shows an exceptional ability to work with other riders.
I've also been informed that he's from Denver.
So he's picked up two wins over the past two days.
